campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to discuss several items including accepting a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending a temporary local ordinance and reviewed plans for improving bicycle and pedestrian safety at the Hamilton Avenue crossing over Highway 17.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth scholarship program
Extension of interim ordinance #2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle and pedestrian overcross project
Grant application for Highway 17 crossing improvements
Feasibility study results for bike/pedestrian safety

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a formal city law) and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance is scheduled to be formally adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited for faster approval
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular meeting
First reading waived to streamline the approval process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve records from several March meetings and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting primarily focused on administrative housekeeping—reviewing and approving minutes from previous sessions—along with moving forward on a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from March 16-17 and March 30
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Closed session discussions (specific topics not detailed in agenda)
Administrative procedural approvals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several important agreements and projects. The council voted on updates to contracts for airport baggage systems, housing affordability protections, storm drain planning, and a family camp project funded by federal disaster relief money.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system upgrade contract
New rules to keep affordable housing affordable if subsidies end
Storm sewer system city-wide planning
FEMA-funded family camp project at 8721

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on large construction and infrastructure projects. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Capital Improvement Program budget for fiscal year 2026-27
Planning for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation and recognition
National Volunteer Week proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These recognitions highlighted the city's commitment to celebrating diverse communities and ensuring fair housing practices for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity celebration
Housing rights and protections

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends money and manages its budget. The council also reviewed rules about lobbyists—people who try to influence city decisions—and what they need to report to keep things transparent.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget review and public engagement
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
How to track city spending and legislative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to take legal action, you have 90 days from when the decision is finalized to file a lawsuit.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure compliance notice

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including reviewing a major update to the city's five-year housing and community development plan for 2025-2030. They also discussed changing rules about how council vacancies are filled and reviewed requests from individual council members for new items to work on.
keyTopics: Update to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (housing and community development strategy)
Changes to council vacancy procedures
Review of council members' workplan requests
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is asking to officially recognize the building as more historically important than it currently is, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space if they fix up the building and make it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade request
5,000 square feet bonus floor area for seismic/historic improvements
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Approval of previous board minutes from March 12
